Note on Field Theory Approach to Dynamics

Chapter 4 of Applications of Statistical Field Theory in Deep Learning

Yuhao

April 1, 2025 @PMI Group Meeting

 

1. Introduction

consider a two-layer fully connected network

(1)f(xμ)=∑iD1Dviϕ(ziμ)ziμ=1N∑jwijxjμ

initialize vi∼N(0,σv2) and wij∼(0,σw2) with σv,σw∼O(1)

loss function

(2)L=LMSE+12κ2σv2ηv2+12κ2σw2ηw2

where

(3)LMSE=12∑μ=1P[f(xμ)−yμ]2

η is the learning rate and κ is the ridge parameter

 

2. Review

2.1. Neural Network Gaussian Process

the output of random initial neural network in the infinite width limit

(4)E[f(x)]=0
(5)KNNGP(x,x′)≡E[f(x)f(x′)]=σv2Ew[ϕ(w⊤x)ϕ(w⊤x′)]
(6)f(x)∼GP(0,KNNGP(x,x′))

Note

this means, for given inputs {x1,⋯,xn}, the outputs f=[f(x1),⋯,f(xn)]⊤ follow

(7)f∼N(0,K)

where Kij=KNNGP(xi,xj)

 

2.2. Gaussian Process Regression

for ridge regression / ℓ2 regularization, the effective kernel reads

(8)Keff=K+κ2I

for a new data point x⋆ the output

(9)P(y⋆∣X,y,x⋆)=N(μ⋆,Σ⋆)
(10)μ⋆=K(x⋆,X)⊤[K(X,X)+κ2I]−1y
(11)Σ⋆=K(x⋆,x⋆)−K(x⋆,X)⊤[K(X,X)+κ2I]−1K(x⋆,X)

if we choose the NNGP kernel, the GPR is equivalent to the trained infinite width neural network

 

1.3. Neural Tangent Kernel Dynamics

gradient flow

(12)df(xμ)dt=∑α∂f(xμ)∂θαdθαdt

where

(13)dθαdt=−η∂L∂θα=−η∑ν∂f(xν)∂θα(f(xν)−y(xν))

so we have

(14)df(xμ)dt=−η∑ν[∑α∂f(xμ)∂θα∂f(xν)∂θα](f(xν)−y(xν))(15)=−η∑νΘ(xμ,xν)(f(xν)−y(xν))

with the time-dependent Neural Tangent Kernel

(16)Θ(xμ,xν)=∑α∂f(xμ)∂θα∂f(xν)∂θα

in the infinite width limit, the NTK does not change with time

 

1.4. MSRDJ Formalism

stochastic differential equation

(17)∂tx=f(x)+ξ(t)
(18)x(0+)=x0x0∼P0
(19)⟨ξ(t)⟩=0⟨ξ(t)ξ(t′)⟩=g2δ(t−t′)

the MSRDJ generating function

(20)Z[j,j~]=∫dx0P0∫D[x,x~]exp⁡(S[x,x~]+Wξ[−x~]+∫dt[j(t)x(t)+j~(t)x~(t)])

where the action reads

(21)S[x,x~]=∫dtx~[∂tx−f(x)]

and for Gaussian noises (Eq. 19), the noise generating function reads

(22)Wξ[−x~]=12g2∫x~2(t)dt

take j=j~=0, the partition function reads

(23)Z=∫D[x,x~,x0] e−S+log⁡(P0)

with the action

(24)S=∫0tdτ [−x~(∂τx(τ)−f(x(τ)))−12g2x~2(τ)]

 

2. Introduction

the time-continuous Langevin dynamics

(25)dvidt=−η∂viL(t)+ξiv(t)
(26)dwijdt=−η∂wijL(t)+ξijw(t)

with

(27)⟨ξiv(t)⟩=0⟨ξiv(t)ξjv(t′)⟩=ηκ2δijδ(t−t′)
(28)⟨ξijw(t)⟩=0⟨ξijw(t)ξklw(t′)⟩=ηκ2δikδjlδ(t−t′)

the purpose of this chapter is to recover the Neural Tangent Kernel and the NNGP kernel through the field theory approach

 

3. MSRDJ Partition Function Approach

partition function

(29)Z=∫D[w,w~,v,v~] e−S1−S2−Sinit

where

(30)−S1=∑i∫0tdτ (12ηκ2v~i2(τ)+v~i(τ)[Lvvi(τ)+η∂viLMSE(τ)])
(31)−S2=∑i,j∫0tdτ (12ηκ2w~ij2(τ)+w~ij(τ)[Lwwij(τ)+η∂wijLMSE(τ)])
(32)Sinit=∑i12σv2vi2(0)+∑i,j12σw2wij2(0)

where we define

(33)Lv=∂τ+ηκ2σv2Lw=∂τ+ηκ2σw2

introduce the definition of f(xμ,t)=fμ(t) by Dirac delta function

(34)1=∏μ=1P∫Dfμ δ(fμ−∑iD1Dviϕ(ziμ))

so we have

(35)∂viLMSE=∑μ1D(fμ−yμ)ϕ(ziμ)
(36)∂wijLMSE=∑μvixjμDN(fμ−yμ)ϕ′(ziμ)

rewrite the partition function as

(37)Z=∫D[w,w~,v,v~,f,f~] e−S−Sinit

with

(38)S=∑i∫0tdτ {−12ηκ2v~i2−v~i[Lvvi+η∑μ1D(fμ−yμ)ϕ(ziμ)]}+∑i,j∫0tdτ {−12ηκ2w~ij2−w~ij[Lwwij+η∑μvixjμDN(fμ−yμ)ϕ′(ziμ)]}+∑μ∫0tdτ {f~μ[fμ−∑iviDϕ(ziμ)]}

integrating over v~i and vi, yields vi∼N(v¯i,Σi) with

(39)v¯i(t)=−η∫0tdt′ e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2∑μ1D(fμ(t′)−yμ)ϕ(ziμ(t′))
(40)Σi(t,t′)=⟨vi(t)vi(t′)⟩=12σv2e−ηκ2|t−t′|/σv2

Note

the mean of vi may be decided by

(41)[∂τ+ηκ2σv2]vi+η∑μ1D(fμ−yμ)ϕ(ziμ)=0

generally,

(42)Lv=f⟹v(t)=vhom+∫0tG(t,t′)f(t′)dt′

Lvhom=0 gives the homogeneous solution

LG(t,t′)=δ(t−t′) gives the Green function of the operator L

L=∂t+λ⟹G(t,t′)=Θ(t−t′)e−λ(t−t′)

the solution of Eq. 41 gives Eq. 39

 

the variance of vi may be decided by the dynamics

(43)[∂τ+ηκ2σv2]v=ξv(t)with⟨ξv(t)⟩=0⟨ξv(t)ξv(t′)⟩=ηκ2δ(t−t′)

which gives

(44)v(t)=∫0tdt′ G(t,t′)ξv(t′)

then we have

(45)Σ(t,t′)=⟨∫0tdτ∫0t′dτ′ G(t,τ)G(t′,τ′)ξv(τ)ξv(τ′)⟩=ηκ2∫0tdτ∫0t′dτ′ G(t,τ)G(t′,τ′)δ(τ−τ′)=ηκ2∫0min(t,t′)dτ G(t,τ)G(t′,τ)=ηκ2∫0min(t,t′)dτ e−ηκ2(2τ−t−t′)/σv2=12σv2e−ηκ2|t−t′|/σv2

we obtained

(46)Z=∫D[w,w~,f,f~] e−S−∑i,jwij2(0)/2σw2

with

(47)S=−∑i,j∫0tdτ {ηκ22χw~ij2+w~ijLwwij+w~ij2η∑μv¯ixjμDN(fμ−yμ)ϕ′(ziμ)}−∑μ∫0tdτ {f~μ[fμ−∑iv¯iDϕ(ziμ)]}−∑i∫0tdτ∫0t′dτ′[−∑μf~μDϕ(ziμ)+η∑jw~ijxjμDN(fμ−yμ)ϕ′(ziμ)]τΣi(τ,τ′)[−∑νf~νDϕ(ziμ)+η∑kw~ikxkνDN(fν−yν)ϕ′(ziμ)]τ′

rewrite the partition function as

(48)Z=∫D[w,w~,f,f~] eSw+Sf=⟨∫D[f,f~] eSf⟩Sw

where Sw is the first line in Eq. 47 added the initial item and Sf is the last three lines in Eq. 47

 

4. Infinite Width Limit

the first approximation

(49)Sw≈∑i,j−12σw2wij2(0)+∫0tdτ {ηκ22χw~ij2+w~ijLwwij}

the second approximation

Warning

apply cumulant expansion for Sf under e−Sw, keeping order zero terms in 1C

(50)Z=∫D[f,f~] eS¯f
(51)S¯f=∑μ,ν∫0tdτ {−f~μ[fμ+η(Lv−1⊙Φ(xμ,xν))(fν−yν)]−η(fμ−yμ)[Σv⊙Lw−1⊙Φ′(xμ,xν)Kx(xμ,xν)]f~ν−f~μ[Σv⊙Φ(xμ,xν)]f~ν}

where M⊙N denote M(t,s)N(t,s), the kernel Kx(xμ,xν)=xμ⋅xν and

(52)Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′)=⟨∑i1Dϕ(ziμ(t))ϕ(ziμ(t′))⟩Sw
(53)Φ′(xμ,xν;t,t′)=⟨∑i1DNϕ′(ziμ(t))ϕ′(ziμ(t′))⟩Sw

Warning

The above action is quadratic in f,f~, allowing us to take the average of fμ(t)

(54)fμ(t)=−∫0tdt′ η∑ν(fν(t′)−yν){e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′)+12σv2e−ηκ2|t−t′|/σv2e−ηκ2|t−t′|/σw2[Φ′(xμ,xν;t,t′)Kx(xμ,xν)]}

5. Recovering

5.1. NTK Dynamics

take the t derivative of Eq. 54

(55)f˙μ(t)=−η∑ν(fν(t)−yν)(Φ(xμ,xν;t,t)+12σv2Φ′(xμ,xν;t,t)Kx(xμ,xν))

the Neural Tangent Kernel reads

(56)Θ(xμ,xν)=Φ(xμ,xν;t,t)+12σv2Φ′(xμ,xν;t,t)Kx(xμ,xν)

Warning

why the authors give Eq. 4.22 in their paper?

 

5.2. NNGP Kernel at equilibrium of GPR

rewrite

(57)e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2=σv2ηκ2∂t′e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2

then

(58)fμ(t)=−∫0tdt′ η∑ν(fν(t′)−yν){σv2ηκ2Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′)∂t′e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2+12σv2e−ηκ2|t−t′|/σv2e−ηκ2|t−t′|/σw2[Φ′(xμ,xν;t,t′)Kx(xμ,xν)]}

Note

integrate by parts

(59)∫u dv=uv−∫v du

it seems that the authors consider only the first item, i.e.,

(60)fμ(t)=−σv2κ2∫0t∑ν(fν(t′)−yν)Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′) de−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2=−σv2κ2e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2∑ν(fν(t′)−yν)Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′)|t′=0t+∑ν∫dt′ [σv2κ2e−ηκ2(t−t′)/σv2Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′)f˙ν(t′)]

Warning

dΦ(xμ,xν;t,t′)(fν(t′)−yν)=Φ(xμ,xν;t,t′)f˙ν(t′)+Φ˙(xμ,xν;t,t′)(fν(t′)−yν)

why Φ˙=0 ?

at the limit t→∞ the item f˙ν(t)=0 so the integral item vanishes

Warning

Leaving us with

(61)fGP(xσ)=∑μ,νKσ,μGP[(KGP+κ2I)−1]μ,νyν

where KμνGP=limt→∞σ2Φ(xμ,xν,t,t) and fGP(xσ)=limt→∞fσ(t)
